failure_summary: V9 did not pass tiny same-set validation. All three ALLOW siblings ended null/no-select because no structurally valid artifact was selectable; all three ESCALATE controls remained correct; no false ALLOW occurred.

mechanism.closure_behavior: Affirmative closure ledgers existed and were SOURCE_CLOSED in name_list mode, but required_field_values were null and value_equality_status was MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELD_VALUE.
mechanism.control_behavior: ESCALATE controls stayed protected because their closure ledgers were SOURCE_OPEN with visible open markers.
mechanism.gov_behavior: Gov preserved blocker IDs forward and instructed later workers not to override blockers unless sources close them.
mechanism.selector_behavior: Selector found no structurally valid artifact for ALLOW siblings and returned final verdict null/no-select.
mechanism.v9_resolution_behavior: deterministic_generic_blocker_resolution_ledger remained empty, so the generic blockers were not cleared.
mechanism.worker_behavior: Workers repeatedly created or preserved generic SCOPE_MISMATCH / exact-match-absent blockers on ALLOW siblings.

next_patch_target_if_supported_by_eviden: A narrow follow-up should examine whether the deterministic closure layer should either extract explicit value tuples from runtime-visible source text or define a guarded name-list closure path for these families. Any patch must preserve V9 value-equality safeguards, source-open E controls, and false-ALLOW avoidance. If value tuples cannot be source-bound, the safer outcome remains fail-closed/null.
